title: UN Chief Warns of Severe Consequences from Prolonged Strait of Hormuz Disruption
summary: UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres warns of devastating economic and humanitarian consequences if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed, calling for immediate restoration of navigational rights under Security Council Resolution 2817.
contents:
## UN Chief Warns of Severe Consequences from Prolonged Strait of Hormuz Disruption

### Overview
UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres warns of devastating economic and humanitarian consequences if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed, calling for immediate restoration of navigational rights under Security Council Resolution 2817.

### Details
Guterres presented three scenarios for prolonged strait disruptions, with the worst-case outcome projecting global inflation exceeding 6 percent and economic growth plummeting to 2 percent by year-end. He emphasized that consequences escalate exponentially rather than cumulatively, threatening global recession and destabilizing vulnerable populations while urging all parties to restore shipping safety and predictability.

### Geographic context
Location: United Nations, New York.
